recipes.name: Best Cocoa Brownies
recipes.unique_factor: Classic fudgy result from melted-butter-and-cocoa method (no melted chocolate bar required) plus 40 vigorous strokes that develop the signature shiny, crackly top.
ingredients: 3/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons (65 grams) unsweetened cocoa powder
2/3 cup (75 grams) walnut or pecan pieces (optional)
steps: Position oven rack in lower third and preheat to 325°F. Line 8×8-inch pan with parchment paper.
Combine butter, sugar, cocoa, and salt in heatproof bowl. Set bowl in wide skillet of barely simmering water, stirring occasionally until melted and smooth. Alternatively, melt in microwave.
Set bowl aside until mixture cools to warm. Stir in vanilla with wooden spoon.
Add eggs one at a time, stirring vigorously after each addition.
When batter looks thick and shiny, add flour and stir until incorporated. Beat vigorously for 40 strokes.
Stir in nuts if using. Spread evenly in lined pan.
Bake until toothpick emerges slightly moist, approximately 20-25 minutes (may require longer).
Cool completely on rack. Chill in refrigerator or freezer for clean cuts.
Lift parchment ends and transfer to cutting board. Cut into 16 or 25 squares.

recipes.name: Our Ultimate Chewy Brownies
recipes.unique_factor: Half-butter / half-vegetable-oil fat split is the chewy-brownie secret — oil stays liquid at room temp, locking in that boxed-mix tear and crackle from a scratch pantry.
ingredients: 3/4 cup (64g) unsweetened cocoa, Dutch-process or natural
8 tablespoons (113g) unsalted butter, melted and cooled
1 cup (120g) King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour
1 teaspoon King Arthur Pure Vanilla Extract
steps: Preheat the oven to 325°F. Lightly grease a metal 9" square pan or, for slightly thicker brownies, an 8" square pan.
Combine all the ingredients in a bowl and mix well.
Spread the batter in the pan.
Bake for 30 to 40 minutes, until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out with some moist crumbs clinging to it and the internal temperature registers 220°F on an instant-read thermometer.
Remove the pan from the oven and cool the brownies completely. For easiest slicing, chill the brownies in the refrigerator for 1 to 2 hours after they've cooled to room temperature.
Store, covered, for 5 days at room temperature, or freeze for up to a month.

recipes.name: Cakey Brownies
recipes.unique_factor: Baking powder plus a 1/2 cup of water lifts these into true tender cake territory — the only brownie that holds a cool glass of milk like a chocolate-cake square should.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon (14g) King Arthur Pure Vanilla Extract
1 1/2 cups (180g) King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour
1 cup (113g) chopped walnuts or pecans, optional
steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Lightly grease a 9" x 13" pan.
Combine the melted butter and sugar, stirring until smooth.
Stir in the cocoa, salt, baking powder, vanilla, and espresso powder.
Beat in the eggs one at a time. Scrape the bowl, then add the water, stirring until smooth.
Add the flour, nuts, and chips, stirring until thoroughly combined.
Spoon the batter into the prepared pan.
Bake for 28 to 30 minutes, until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, or with a few moist crumbs attached.
Remove from the oven and cool on a rack before cutting and serving.

recipes.name: Tahini Brownies
recipes.unique_factor: Half the batter is reserved as a pure tahini-egg-sugar mixture and dolloped + swirled on top — gives a marbled nutty halva-like ribbon through every fudgy square.
ingredients: 4 tbsp salted butter plus more for the skillet
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees F. Line an 8-inch square pan with 2 pieces of foil (crossed, and with excess hanging over on all sides of the pan). Lightly brush the foil with butter.
Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Remove from heat and immediately add the chocolate chips and cocoa. Whisk until smooth.

In a large mixing bowl, whisk the eggs, sugar, vanilla and salt to form a slightly thick mixture. Add the tahini and whisk to combine. Now fold in the flour, stir until just incorporated (do not over-stir). Reserve half of the tahini mixture in a separate bowl for later. Add the melted chocolate mixture to the remaining tahini mixture, stir until batter is well combined.
Pour the batter into the pan you prepared. Spread evenly.

With a spoon, dollop the reserved tahini mixture over the top (imagine a grid, and add one dollop of tahini mixture to each square on that grid). With the tip of a butter or pairing knife, swirl the tahini dollops into the brownie batter.

Place the pan on the middle rack of the 350 degrees F heated-oven. Bake for 28-30 minutes, until the edges are set but the center remain moist. Remove from the oven. Cool in the pan for 30 minutes. Lift the foil edges up carefully to transfer the brownies from the pan. Cool for another 30 minutes (do not remove from the foil yet). Cut into 2-inch squares. Enjoy!

recipes.name: Quick and Easy Brownies
recipes.unique_factor: Pure dump-and-stir pantry method — every ingredient goes into one bowl in order with zero pre-melting or mixer, ready in 25 minutes flat with optional espresso powder for hidden depth.
ingredients: 1 cup (120g) King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour
3/4 cup (64g) unsweetened cocoa, Dutch-process or natural
2 teaspoons King Arthur Pure Vanilla Extract
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F. Lightly grease a 9" x 13" pan.
In a large bowl, place all of the ingredients in the order listed. Stir, then beat the mixture until it's smooth.
Spoon the batter into the prepared pan.

Bake the brownies for about 25 minutes, or until they're just barely beginning to pull away from the sides of the pan. A toothpick inserted into the center will come out clean or with a few moist crumbs clinging to it; you shouldn't see any wet batter.
Remove the brownies from the oven; let them cool completely before cutting.
Store any leftover brownies, well-wrapped, at room temperature for 5 to 6 days. Freeze for longer storage.
